steps.title: Identify the claim to support
steps.content: Stöger and her team claim that giraffes use humming sounds to communicate when visual signaling is impossible. Any finding that directly links the humming to situations where giraffes cannot see each other would support this claim.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Giraffes have an excellent sense of vision and can see in color.’: Describes giraffes’ vision quality but says nothing about when humming occurs or its communicative function. Irrelevant to the claim. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘The giraffes only produced the humming sounds at night when they couldn’t see one another.’: Directly ties humming to the condition where visual signaling is impossible (nighttime, no visibility). Strongly supports the claim. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘Wild giraffes have never been recorded making humming sounds.’: Would weaken the claim by casting doubt on whether the behavior exists outside zoos, not support it. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Researchers observed other animals in European zoos humming.’: Concerns other species, not giraffes. Provides no evidence about why giraffes hum or when. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
